input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: Validation evidence is a single operating point; the COU spans a range.
fired_pattern.standard_anchor: ASME V&V 40 application-domain coverage; proto-taxonomy D-VAL-08

case_context.cou: surrogate predicts flame temperature across an equivalence-ratio sweep

answer_key.gold_mechanism: A single-point validation, however accurate, does not cover a COU that sweeps a wide, nonlinear equivalence-ratio range; coverage of the decision domain is not established.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

forbidden_claims: asserts the swept predictions are wrong (a verdict one point does not support)
treats single-point accuracy as range coverage

hardness.obvious_signal: validation against experiment is excellent (0.5% error) and well-documented
hardness.conflicting_signal: that validation is at a SINGLE operating point (one equivalence ratio), but the COU sweeps equivalence ratio across a wide flammability range where the chemistry is highly nonlinear — one point does not cover the swept range

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 application-domain coverage: validation must span the COU's domain; a single point does not cover a nonlinear swept range.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
